answer:
Sketch:
- 10 pages/wk, mostly false → alert noise is root cause
- 6-person rotation is fine (on-call 1 week in 6)
- Burnout driver = interrupt volume, not schedule
- Fix signal before touching rotation

Answer: Fix the alerts first, not the rotation. A 6-person weekly rotation is healthy; ~10 pages a week where most are false alarms is not. Do an alert audit: for every page in the last month, ask "was it actionable and urgent?" If no — delete it, downgrade it to a ticket/dashboard, or fix the threshold. Enforce a rule going forward: every page must be actionable, or it gets tuned or killed in the next review (a weekly 30-minute "page review" ritual works well). Target: fewer than 2–3 pages per on-call week.

Only after the noise is gone should you consider structural changes (follow-the-sun, secondary on-call, comp for pages). If you shuffle the rotation now, you're just redistributing the same misery. Alert fatigue is the fastest, cheapest thing to fix and typically cuts pages 70%+ in a few weeks.
